title: Multi-Task Next-Pit Horizon Stacking
summary: A recent lap-level pit-stop paper used a cascaded multi-task setup where next-lap classification is complemented by a regression estimate of laps remaining until stop.
rationale: A recent lap-level pit-stop paper used a cascaded multi-task setup where next-lap classification is complemented by a regression estimate of laps remaining until stop. That is a natural extension here because ROC AUC can improve when the model learns a smoother hazard progression instead of only a one-step binary label.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary target equal to remaining laps until the next PitStop within each driver-race sequence, censored when no later stop exists. Train a separate regression or ordinal model OOF, then use its prediction, rank, and clipped inverse-horizon transforms as features for the final binary model.
expected_effect: Provides complementary supervision that smooths noisy positives and sharpens the ramp-up into pit windows.
risk: Censoring and final-stint handling matter; bad label construction can inject noise or leak sequence endpoints across folds.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
